saved_test.name: Nominal Style and Information Density: V3 All Levels
saved_test.description: Page-local V3 package for Nominal Style and Information Density with A1-C2 coverage.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for nominal style: {a1}

a1.answer: The report uses a formal nominal style
options: The report uses a formal nominal style
The report uses a very casual style
The report uses style formal nominal
The report is using got a style
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for information density: {a1}

a1.answer: The paragraph contains a high density of information
options: The paragraph contains a high density of information
The paragraph contains many words with no information
The paragraph has information high density
The paragraph is too casual for data
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for compact noun phrasing: {a1}

a1.answer: The lack of affordable housing remains a serious problem
options: The lack of affordable housing remains a serious problem
Housing is not enough and this is bad
The housing affordable lack remains problem serious
People do not have houses and it is a thing
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for noun phrase compression: {a1}

a1.answer: The rapid increase in prices affected demand
options: The rapid increase in prices affected demand
Prices went up fast and demand changed
The rapid increasing prices affected demand
The prices rapid increase demand affected
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for verb phrase to nominal phrase: {a1}

a1.answer: The analysis of the data changed the recommendation
options: The analysis of the data changed the recommendation
When they analysed the data they changed it
The analyse of the data changed recommendation
The data analysed changed of recommendation
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for premodification and postmodification: {a1}

a1.answer: The recently published safety report raised concerns
options: The recently published safety report raised concerns
The safety report that was recently published raised concerns
The published recently report safety raised concerns
The report safety recent raised concerns
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for apposition and clarification: {a1}

a1.answer: The director a senior economist explained the figures
options: The director a senior economist explained the figures
The director explained the figures and he is senior
The senior director economist explained figures the
The director seniorly economic explained the figures
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for academic noun groups: {a1}

a1.answer: The long term impact of climate policy remains unclear
options: The long term impact of climate policy remains unclear
Climate policy impacts things for a long time
The impact long term of climate policy remains unclear
The climate policy long impact unclear remains
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for concise formal information flow: {a1}

a1.answer: Following data analysis the team revised its recommendations
options: Following data analysis the team revised its recommendations
After they analysed the data the team changed what it said
Following data analysing the team revised recommendations its
The team revised following its data analysis recommendations
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for avoiding overloaded noun phrases: {a1}

a1.answer: The study explains the main causes clearly
options: The study explains the main causes clearly
The study gives an explanation of the causation complexity clearly
The study explanation main causes clearly
The causes main explains the study clearly
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for balancing clarity and density: {a1}

a1.answer: The summary is compact but clear
options: The summary is compact but clear
The summary is dense and impossible to read
The compact summary but clear is
The summary has compactness with clearness
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.

questions.question: Choose the natural formal-English option for typical mistakes: {a1}

a1.answer: The reduction in costs was significant
options: The reduction in costs was significant
Costs reduced was significant
The reduce of costs was significant
The costs reduction were significant
a1.verb_hint: Choose the option that preserves meaning and formal register.
